sz_consts.name: ENCRYPTED_TAG_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the authentication tag appended to each encrypted chunk by the AEAD cipher (GCM/Poly1305). This overhead must be accounted for when sizing encrypted output buffers.

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_SEED_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the private seed used to deterministically derive an ML-DSA keypair.

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_44_PUBKEY_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of an ML-DSA-44 public (verifier) key.

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_65_PUBKEY_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of an ML-DSA-65 public (verifier) key.

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_87_PUBKEY_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of an ML-DSA-87 public (verifier) key.

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_44_SIGNATURE_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of a detached signature produced by ML-DSA-44.

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_65_SIGNATURE_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of a detached signature produced by ML-DSA-65.

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_87_SIGNATURE_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of a detached signature produced by ML-DSA-87.

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_MU_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the message representative mu, the internal hash of the message used during signing and verification.

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_MU_RAND_SEED_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the randomness seed used during computation of mu, providing hedged signing.

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_44_KEYPAIR_SZ
sz_consts.description: Total size in bytes of a serialized ML-DSA-44 keypair (private seed concatenated with public key).

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_65_KEYPAIR_SZ
sz_consts.description: Total size in bytes of a serialized ML-DSA-65 keypair (private seed concatenated with public key).

sz_consts.name: MLDSA_87_KEYPAIR_SZ
sz_consts.description: Total size in bytes of a serialized ML-DSA-87 keypair (private seed concatenated with public key).

byte_aliases.name: MlDsa44KeypairRaw
byte_aliases.description: Raw byte representation of an ML-DSA-44 keypair. Contains the private seed followed by the public key.

byte_aliases.name: MlDsa65KeypairRaw
byte_aliases.description: Raw byte representation of an ML-DSA-65 keypair. Contains the private seed followed by the public key.

byte_aliases.name: MlDsa87KeypairRaw
byte_aliases.description: Raw byte representation of an ML-DSA-87 keypair. Contains the private seed followed by the public key.

byte_aliases.name: MlDsaPrivateSeed
byte_aliases.description: Secret seed bytes from which an ML-DSA keypair is deterministically derived. Must be generated from a cryptographically secure random source and kept confidential.

byte_aliases.name: MlDsa44Pubkey
byte_aliases.description: Public key bytes for ML-DSA-44. Safe to share openly; used by verifiers to authenticate signatures.

byte_aliases.name: MlDsa65Pubkey
byte_aliases.description: Public key bytes for ML-DSA-65. Safe to share openly; used by verifiers to authenticate signatures.

byte_aliases.name: MlDsa87Pubkey
byte_aliases.description: Public key bytes for ML-DSA-87. Safe to share openly; used by verifiers to authenticate signatures.

byte_aliases.name: MlDsa44Signature
byte_aliases.description: Detached signature bytes produced by ML-DSA-44. Transmitted alongside the original message to allow third-party verification.

byte_aliases.name: MlDsa65Signature
byte_aliases.description: Detached signature bytes produced by ML-DSA-65. Transmitted alongside the original message to allow third-party verification.

byte_aliases.name: MlDsa87Signature
byte_aliases.description: Detached signature bytes produced by ML-DSA-87. Transmitted alongside the original message to allow third-party verification.

byte_aliases.name: MlDsaMuRandSeed
byte_aliases.description: Randomness seed injected into mu computation for hedged (randomized) signing, reducing risk from deterministic signing under fault attacks.

byte_aliases.name: MlDsaMu
byte_aliases.description: Message representative produced by hashing the message (and optional context) prior to signing. Intermediate value used internally by the ML-DSA algorithm.

empty_consts.name: EMPTY_MLDSA_SEED
empty_consts.description: Zero-initialized ML-DSA private seed. Provided as a convenient stack-allocated buffer to fill before keypair derivation; must never be used as an actual key.

structs.name: MlDsa44Keypair
structs.description: An ML-DSA-44 keypair holding both the private seed and the derived public key. The security level targets NIST Category 2. Use for signing operations where moderate signature size and high performance are preferred.

fields.name: private_seed
fields.description: Secret 32-byte seed from which the full ML-DSA-44 signing key is derived. Keep confidential; exposure allows signature forgery.

deprecated_fields.name: public_key
deprecated_fields.description: Derived ML-DSA-44 public key. May be published or transmitted to verifiers.

structs.name: MlDsa65Keypair
structs.description: An ML-DSA-65 keypair holding both the private seed and the derived public key. Targets NIST Category 3, providing a higher security margin than ML-DSA-44 at the cost of larger key and signature sizes.

fields.name: private_seed
fields.description: Secret 32-byte seed from which the full ML-DSA-65 signing key is derived. Keep confidential; exposure allows signature forgery.

deprecated_fields.name: public_key
deprecated_fields.description: Derived ML-DSA-65 public key. May be published or transmitted to verifiers.

structs.name: MlDsa87Keypair
structs.description: An ML-DSA-87 keypair holding both the private seed and the derived public key. Targets NIST Category 5, the highest security level, with the largest key and signature sizes.

fields.name: private_seed
fields.description: Secret 32-byte seed from which the full ML-DSA-87 signing key is derived. Keep confidential; exposure allows signature forgery.

deprecated_fields.name: public_key
deprecated_fields.description: Derived ML-DSA-87 public key. May be published or transmitted to verifiers.

structs.name: MlDsa44Verifier
structs.description: A standalone ML-DSA-44 verifier holding only the public key. Share this with parties that need to verify signatures but must not have access to the private seed.

fields.name: public_key
fields.description: ML-DSA-44 public key used exclusively for signature verification.

structs.name: MlDsa65Verifier
structs.description: A standalone ML-DSA-65 verifier holding only the public key. Share this with parties that need to verify signatures but must not have access to the private seed.

fields.name: public_key
fields.description: ML-DSA-65 public key used exclusively for signature verification.

structs.name: MlDsa87Verifier
structs.description: A standalone ML-DSA-87 verifier holding only the public key. Share this with parties that need to verify signatures but must not have access to the private seed.

fields.name: public_key
fields.description: ML-DSA-87 public key used exclusively for signature verification.

traits.name: MlDsaKeypair
traits.description: Common serialization interface shared by all ML-DSA keypair variants. Provides seed-based construction and round-trip byte serialization.

functions.name: pack_bytes
functions.description: Returns a reference to the raw private seed bytes. The public key can be re-derived from these bytes at any time, so only the seed needs to be persisted.

functions.name: unpack_bytes
functions.description: Reconstructs a keypair from previously serialized private seed bytes. Deterministic: the same seed always produces the same keypair.

params.name: seed_bytes
params.description: Raw private seed bytes previously obtained from pack.

functions.name: from_seed
functions.description: Derives a new keypair from a provided seed. Deterministic: the same seed always produces the same keypair. The seed must originate from a cryptographically secure random source.

params.name: seed_bytes
params.description: A 32-byte secret seed generated from a CSPRNG.

traits.name: IMlDsa44Pubkey
traits.description: Byte serialization interface for ML-DSA-44 public keys, enabling storage and transmission.

functions.name: pack_bytes
functions.description: Returns a reference to the raw ML-DSA-44 public key bytes. Safe to transmit or store publicly.

functions.name: unpack_bytes
functions.description: Constructs an ML-DSA-44 public key object from raw bytes.

params.name: bytes
params.description: Raw public key bytes of exactly MLDSA_44_PUBKEY_SZ length.

traits.name: IMlDsa65Pubkey
traits.description: Byte serialization interface for ML-DSA-65 public keys, enabling storage and transmission.

functions.name: pack_bytes
functions.description: Returns a reference to the raw ML-DSA-65 public key bytes. Safe to transmit or store publicly.

functions.name: unpack_bytes
functions.description: Constructs an ML-DSA-65 public key object from raw bytes.

params.name: bytes
params.description: Raw public key bytes of exactly MLDSA_65_PUBKEY_SZ length.

traits.name: IMlDsa87Pubkey
traits.description: Byte serialization interface for ML-DSA-87 public keys, enabling storage and transmission.

functions.name: pack_bytes
functions.description: Returns a reference to the raw ML-DSA-87 public key bytes. Safe to transmit or store publicly.

functions.name: unpack_bytes
functions.description: Constructs an ML-DSA-87 public key object from raw bytes.

params.name: bytes
params.description: Raw public key bytes of exactly MLDSA_87_PUBKEY_SZ length.

traits.name: IMlDsa44Keypair
traits.description: Signing interface for ML-DSA-44 keypairs. Provides message signing and verifier extraction.

functions.name: sign
functions.description: Signs an arbitrary byte message using the ML-DSA-44 private key and returns a detached signature. The signature can be verified by any holder of the corresponding public key.

params.name: msg
params.description: The message bytes to sign. May be of any length.

functions.name: get_verifier
functions.description: Extracts a standalone MlDsa44Verifier containing only the public key. Use this to share verification capability without exposing the private seed.

traits.name: IMlDsa65Keypair
traits.description: Signing interface for ML-DSA-65 keypairs. Provides message signing and verifier extraction.

functions.name: sign
functions.description: Signs an arbitrary byte message using the ML-DSA-65 private key and returns a detached signature.

params.name: msg
params.description: The message bytes to sign. May be of any length.

functions.name: get_verifier
functions.description: Extracts a standalone MlDsa65Verifier containing only the public key.

traits.name: IMlDsa87Keypair
traits.description: Signing interface for ML-DSA-87 keypairs. Provides message signing and verifier extraction.

functions.name: sign
functions.description: Signs an arbitrary byte message using the ML-DSA-87 private key and returns a detached signature.

params.name: msg
params.description: The message bytes to sign. May be of any length.

functions.name: get_verifier
functions.description: Extracts a standalone MlDsa87Verifier containing only the public key.

traits.name: IMlDsa44Verifier
traits.description: Signature verification interface for ML-DSA-44. Implemented by both MlDsa44Verifier (public-key-only) and MlDsa44Keypair (which also holds the public key).

functions.name: verify
functions.description: Verifies that a detached ML-DSA-44 signature is valid for the given message under this public key. Returns true if the signature is authentic and unmodified, false otherwise. Does not throw or panic on invalid signatures.

params.name: msg
params.description: The original message bytes that were signed.

params.name: signature
params.description: The detached signature bytes to verify, of exactly MLDSA_44_SIGNATURE_SZ length.

traits.name: IMlDsa65Verifier
traits.description: Signature verification interface for ML-DSA-65.

functions.name: verify
functions.description: Verifies that a detached ML-DSA-65 signature is valid for the given message under this public key. Returns true if authentic, false otherwise.

params.name: msg
params.description: The original message bytes that were signed.

params.name: signature
params.description: The detached signature bytes to verify, of exactly MLDSA_65_SIGNATURE_SZ length.

traits.name: IMlDsa87Verifier
traits.description: Signature verification interface for ML-DSA-87.

functions.name: verify
functions.description: Verifies that a detached ML-DSA-87 signature is valid for the given message under this public key. Returns true if authentic, false otherwise.

params.name: msg
params.description: The original message bytes that were signed.

params.name: signature
params.description: The detached signature bytes to verify, of exactly MLDSA_87_SIGNATURE_SZ length.

sz_consts.name: HASH_KEY_256_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of a 256-bit hash output.

sz_consts.name: HMAC_KEY_256_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of a 256-bit HMAC output.

byte_aliases.name: Hash256
byte_aliases.description: A 256-bit (32-byte) hash digest. Used as a general-purpose key derivation or integrity output.

byte_aliases.name: Hmac256
byte_aliases.description: A 256-bit (32-byte) HMAC digest. Used as a general-purpose key derivation or integrity output.

functions.name: hash
functions.description: Computes a domain-separated salted hash of the input keying material. The domain string and salt are mixed in to prevent cross-context collisions. Use when you need a general-purpose hash bound to a specific protocol role.

params.name: salt
params.description: A per-invocation or per-entity salt value that randomizes the hash output.

params.name: ikm
params.description: Input keying material: the primary data to be hashed.

params.name: domain_info
params.description: A string constant identifying the protocol context or purpose (e.g. "session-key-derive"). Must be unique per distinct usage to prevent cross-domain attacks.

functions.name: hmac
functions.description: Computes a domain-separated HMAC over the input keying material, using the salt as the HMAC key. Provides message authentication in addition to hashing.

params.name: salt
params.description: Value used as the HMAC key material.

params.name: ikm
params.description: Input keying material to authenticate.

params.name: domain_info
params.description: Domain separation string to scope the HMAC output to a specific protocol context.

functions.name: salted_hmac
functions.description: Computes an HMAC using only a salt and input keying material, without domain separation. Prefer hmac() when a domain string is available.

params.name: salt
params.description: Value used as the HMAC key material.

params.name: ikm
params.description: Input keying material to authenticate.

functions.name: domain_hmac
functions.description: Computes an HMAC over input keying material with domain separation but no external salt. The domain string acts as the only differentiator between usages.

params.name: ikm
params.description: Input keying material to authenticate.

params.name: domain_info
params.description: Domain separation string identifying the protocol context.

functions.name: salted_hash
functions.description: Computes a hash of input keying material mixed with a salt, without domain separation.

params.name: salt
params.description: Per-invocation salt value mixed into the hash.

params.name: ikm
params.description: Input keying material to hash.

functions.name: domain_hash
functions.description: Computes a domain-separated hash of the input keying material with no external salt. Useful for deterministic derivation within a fixed protocol context.

params.name: ikm
params.description: Input keying material to hash.

params.name: domain_info
params.description: Domain separation string identifying the protocol context.

functions.name: simple_hmac
functions.description: Computes a bare HMAC over the input with no salt or domain separation. Provided for low-level or legacy use; prefer salted or domain-separated variants in new protocols.

params.name: ikm
params.description: Input keying material to authenticate.

functions.name: simple_hash
functions.description: Computes a bare hash of the input with no salt or domain separation. Provided for low-level or legacy use; prefer salted or domain-separated variants in new protocols.

params.name: ikm
params.description: Input data to hash.

sz_consts.name: MLKEM_PRK_SEED_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the pseudorandom key (PRK) seed passed to the encapsulator to deterministically derive the shared secret and ciphertext.

sz_consts.name: MLKEM_SECRET_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the shared secret produced by both encapsulation and decapsulation. Both sides obtain this same 32-byte value on success.

sz_consts.name: MLKEM_512_CIPHER_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the ML-KEM-512 encapsulated ciphertext transmitted from encapsulator to decapsulator.

sz_consts.name: MLKEM_768_CIPHER_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the ML-KEM-768 encapsulated ciphertext.

sz_consts.name: MLKEM_1024_CIPHER_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the ML-KEM-1024 encapsulated ciphertext.

sz_consts.name: MLKEM_512_ENCAP_KEY_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the ML-KEM-512 public encapsulation key.

sz_consts.name: MLKEM_768_ENCAP_KEY_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the ML-KEM-768 public encapsulation key.

sz_consts.name: MLKEM_1024_ENCAP_KEY_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the ML-KEM-1024 public encapsulation key.

sz_consts.name: MLKEM_DECAP_KEY_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the private decapsulation seed from which the full ML-KEM decapsulation key is derived.

empty_consts.name: EMPTY_KEM_PRK_SEED
empty_consts.description: Zero-initialized PRK seed buffer. Intended as a convenience allocation target; must be filled with CSPRNG output before use.

empty_consts.name: EMPTY_KEM_DECAP_SEED
empty_consts.description: Zero-initialized decapsulation seed buffer. Intended as a convenience allocation target; must be filled with CSPRNG output before use.

byte_aliases.name: MlKemPrkSeed
byte_aliases.description: Pseudorandom key seed provided to the encapsulator. Determines the shared secret and ciphertext deterministically; must come from a CSPRNG.

byte_aliases.name: MlKem512Pubkey
byte_aliases.description: ML-KEM-512 public encapsulation key. Shared with the encapsulating party so they can produce a ciphertext and shared secret.

byte_aliases.name: MlKem768Pubkey
byte_aliases.description: ML-KEM-768 public encapsulation key.

byte_aliases.name: MlKem1024Pubkey
byte_aliases.description: ML-KEM-1024 public encapsulation key.

byte_aliases.name: MlKemPrivateSeed
byte_aliases.description: Secret seed from which the ML-KEM decapsulation key is derived. Keep confidential; exposure allows decapsulation of any ciphertext addressed to this keypair.

byte_aliases.name: MlKemSecret
byte_aliases.description: 32-byte shared secret established by KEM encapsulation/decapsulation. Both parties derive this same value; use it as key material for a symmetric cipher.

byte_aliases.name: MlKem512Cipher
byte_aliases.description: ML-KEM-512 ciphertext produced by encapsulation and sent to the decapsulating party.

byte_aliases.name: MlKem768Cipher
byte_aliases.description: ML-KEM-768 ciphertext produced by encapsulation.

byte_aliases.name: MlKem1024Cipher
byte_aliases.description: ML-KEM-1024 ciphertext produced by encapsulation.

structs.name: MlKem512Keypair
structs.description: An ML-KEM-512 keypair. Targets NIST Category 1. Holds the private decapsulation seed and the derived public encapsulation key. The public key is shared with encapsulating peers; the private seed is kept secret.

fields.name: private_seed
fields.description: Secret decapsulation seed. Keep confidential.

fields.name: public_key
fields.description: Public encapsulation key. Safe to distribute to any party that needs to establish a shared secret with the holder of this keypair.

structs.name: MlKem768Keypair
structs.description: An ML-KEM-768 keypair. Targets NIST Category 3, balancing security and performance. Holds the private decapsulation seed and the derived public encapsulation key.

fields.name: private_seed
fields.description: Secret decapsulation seed. Keep confidential.

fields.name: public_key
fields.description: Public encapsulation key. Safe to distribute.

structs.name: MlKem1024Keypair
structs.description: An ML-KEM-1024 keypair. Targets NIST Category 5, the highest security level. Holds the private decapsulation seed and the derived public encapsulation key.

fields.name: private_seed
fields.description: Secret decapsulation seed. Keep confidential.

fields.name: public_key
fields.description: Public encapsulation key. Safe to distribute.

structs.name: MlKem512EncapsulatorResult
structs.description: Output of an ML-KEM-512 encapsulation operation. Contains both the shared secret (retained locally) and the ciphertext (sent to the decapsulating peer).

fields.name: secret
fields.description: The 32-byte shared secret established by this encapsulation. Do not transmit; use as symmetric key material.

fields.name: cipher
fields.description: The ciphertext to transmit to the decapsulating peer, who will use it to derive the same shared secret.

structs.name: MlKem768EncapsulatorResult
structs.description: Output of an ML-KEM-768 encapsulation operation.

fields.name: secret
fields.description: The 32-byte shared secret. Retain locally as key material; do not transmit.

fields.name: cipher
fields.description: The ciphertext to send to the decapsulating peer.

structs.name: MlKem1024EncapsulatorResult
structs.description: Output of an ML-KEM-1024 encapsulation operation.

fields.name: secret
fields.description: The 32-byte shared secret. Retain locally as key material; do not transmit.

fields.name: cipher
fields.description: The ciphertext to send to the decapsulating peer.

structs.name: MlKem512Encapsulator
structs.description: Holds an ML-KEM-512 public key and performs encapsulation on behalf of a sender. Constructed from a peer's public key; does not contain any private material.

fields.name: public_key
fields.description: The recipient's ML-KEM-512 public encapsulation key.

structs.name: MlKem768Encapsulator
structs.description: Holds an ML-KEM-768 public key and performs encapsulation on behalf of a sender.

fields.name: public_key
fields.description: The recipient's ML-KEM-768 public encapsulation key.

structs.name: MlKem1024Encapsulator
structs.description: Holds an ML-KEM-1024 public key and performs encapsulation on behalf of a sender.

fields.name: public_key
fields.description: The recipient's ML-KEM-1024 public encapsulation key.

traits.name: IMlKem512Keypair
traits.description: Core KEM operations for an ML-KEM-512 keypair: decapsulation and encapsulator extraction.

functions.name: decapsulate
functions.description: Decapsulates a received ciphertext using the private decapsulation key, recovering the shared secret originally established by the encapsulator. The returned secret will be equal to the encapsulator's secret if the ciphertext is authentic.

params.name: encapsulated_key
params.description: The ML-KEM-512 ciphertext received from the encapsulating peer.

functions.name: get_encapsulator
functions.description: Constructs a standalone MlKem512Encapsulator from this keypair's public key, suitable for sharing with peers who need to initiate key establishment without holding the private seed.

traits.name: IMlKem768Keypair
traits.description: Core KEM operations for an ML-KEM-768 keypair.

functions.name: decapsulate
functions.description: Decapsulates a received ML-KEM-768 ciphertext, recovering the shared secret.

params.name: encapsulated_key
params.description: The ML-KEM-768 ciphertext received from the encapsulating peer.

functions.name: get_encapsulator
functions.description: Constructs a standalone MlKem768Encapsulator from this keypair's public key.

traits.name: IMlKem1024Keypair
traits.description: Core KEM operations for an ML-KEM-1024 keypair.

functions.name: decapsulate
functions.description: Decapsulates a received ML-KEM-1024 ciphertext, recovering the shared secret.

params.name: encapsulated_key
params.description: The ML-KEM-1024 ciphertext received from the encapsulating peer.

functions.name: get_encapsulator
functions.description: Constructs a standalone MlKem1024Encapsulator from this keypair's public key.

traits.name: IMlKem512Encapsulator
traits.description: Encapsulation interface for ML-KEM-512. Used by the initiating party to establish a shared secret with the holder of an ML-KEM-512 keypair.

functions.name: encapsulate
functions.description: Encapsulates a fresh shared secret using the recipient's public key and a caller-supplied PRK seed. Returns both the shared secret (kept locally) and a ciphertext (sent to the recipient). The same PRK seed always produces the same output given the same public key; use a fresh CSPRNG-derived seed per session.

params.name: prk
params.description: A 32-byte pseudorandom key seed from a CSPRNG, used to deterministically derive the shared secret and ciphertext.

traits.name: IMlKem768Encapsulator
traits.description: Encapsulation interface for ML-KEM-768.

functions.name: encapsulate
functions.description: Encapsulates a fresh shared secret using the recipient's ML-KEM-768 public key and a PRK seed. Returns the shared secret and ciphertext.

params.name: prk
params.description: A 32-byte pseudorandom key seed from a CSPRNG.

traits.name: IMlKem1024Encapsulator
traits.description: Encapsulation interface for ML-KEM-1024.

functions.name: encapsulate
functions.description: Encapsulates a fresh shared secret using the recipient's ML-KEM-1024 public key and a PRK seed. Returns the shared secret and ciphertext.

params.name: prk
params.description: A 32-byte pseudorandom key seed from a CSPRNG.

traits.name: MlKemKeypair
traits.description: Common serialization interface shared by all ML-KEM keypair variants.

functions.name: pack_bytes
functions.description: Returns a reference to the raw private decapsulation seed bytes. Only the seed needs to be persisted; the public key can be re-derived from it.

functions.name: unpack_bytes
functions.description: Reconstructs an ML-KEM keypair from previously serialized private seed bytes.

params.name: seed_bytes
params.description: Raw private decapsulation seed previously obtained from pack.

functions.name: from_seed
functions.description: Derives a new ML-KEM keypair from a provided seed. The same seed always yields the same keypair.

params.name: seed_bytes
params.description: A secret seed from a CSPRNG used to derive the keypair.

sz_consts.name: PASSWORD_CIPHER_CHUNK_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of each plaintext chunk processed per encrypt/decrypt call. Data is streamed through the cipher in fixed-size blocks of this length.

sz_consts.name: PASSWORD_NONCE_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the nonce used to initialize the password cipher. Must be unique per encryption operation for a given key.

empty_consts.name: EMPTY_PBKDF_KEY
empty_consts.description: Zero-initialized PBKDF key buffer. Provided as a convenience allocation target; must be filled with a derived key before use.

empty_consts.name: EMPTY_PASSWORD_NONCE
empty_consts.description: Zero-initialized nonce buffer. Provided as a convenience allocation target; must be filled with random bytes before use.

byte_aliases.name: PasswordEncryptedChunk
byte_aliases.description: A fixed-size buffer holding one encrypted chunk plus its authentication tag (PASSWORD_CIPHER_CHUNK_SZ + ENCRYPTED_TAG_SZ bytes total).

byte_aliases.name: PasswordUnencryptedChunk
byte_aliases.description: A fixed-size buffer holding one plaintext chunk of PASSWORD_CIPHER_CHUNK_SZ bytes.

byte_aliases.name: PasswordNonce
byte_aliases.description: Nonce used to initialize the password cipher. Must be unique for each encryption operation performed under the same key. Typically generated from a CSPRNG and stored alongside the ciphertext.

structs.name: PasswordCipher
structs.description: Streaming AEAD cipher state for password-based encryption and decryption. Initialized once with a derived key and nonce, then advanced chunk-by-chunk. The counter field tracks position in the stream; n_bytes and chunk_sz track how much data remains.

fields.name: key
fields.description: The 32-byte symmetric key derived from a password via PBKDF. Must be kept confidential for the lifetime of the cipher.

fields.name: nonce
fields.description: The 12-byte nonce that initializes the AEAD cipher. Must be unique per key; typically stored alongside the ciphertext.

fields.name: counter
fields.description: Current chunk index, incremented automatically after each encrypt or decrypt call. Do not modify externally.

fields.name: n_bytes
fields.description: Total number of bytes in the input data being processed, used to determine when the final partial chunk is reached.

fields.name: chunk_sz
fields.description: Number of bytes per chunk; typically equal to PASSWORD_CIPHER_CHUNK_SZ. Set during initialization based on the input length.

structs.name: EncryptChunkResult
structs.description: Result of a single streaming encryption step. Contains the updated cipher state, the encrypted output chunk, and a completion flag.

fields.name: cipher
fields.description: The updated cipher state after encrypting this chunk. Pass to the next encrypt call to continue streaming.

fields.name: next_chunk
fields.description: The encrypted and authenticated output chunk. Append this to the ciphertext output buffer.

fields.name: is_done
fields.description: Non-zero when this is the final chunk and encryption is complete. The calling loop should break on this condition.

structs.name: DecryptChunkResult
structs.description: Result of a single streaming decryption step. Contains the updated cipher state, the decrypted plaintext chunk, the number of valid bytes in it, and a completion flag.

fields.name: cipher
fields.description: The updated cipher state after decrypting this chunk. Pass to the next decrypt call to continue streaming.

fields.name: next_chunk
fields.description: Buffer containing the decrypted plaintext. Only the first n_bytes are valid output; the remainder may be padding.

fields.name: n_bytes
fields.description: Number of valid plaintext bytes in next_chunk for this step. On intermediate chunks this equals PASSWORD_CIPHER_CHUNK_SZ; on the final chunk it may be less.

fields.name: is_done
fields.description: Non-zero when this is the final chunk and decryption is complete. The calling loop should break on this condition.

traits.name: IPasswordCipher
traits.description: Constructor interface for PasswordCipher. Initializes the streaming cipher state from input data metadata, a derived key, and a nonce.

functions.name: new
functions.description: Initializes a PasswordCipher for streaming encryption or decryption. The data slice is used to determine the total byte count and chunk sizing; its contents are not read during initialization. Call encrypt_next or decrypt_next repeatedly until is_done is set.

params.name: data
params.description: A slice over the full input data. Used to derive chunk count and sizing; the cipher does not retain a reference to this data.

params.name: key
params.description: A 32-byte symmetric key produced by a PBKDF. Must match the key used during the corresponding encrypt or decrypt operation.

params.name: nonce
params.description: A 12-byte nonce that must be unique for each encryption performed under the same key. Reusing a nonce with the same key breaks confidentiality.

sz_consts.name: PBKDF_KEY_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the derived symmetric key output from all PBKDF functions.

empty_consts.name: EMPTY_PBKDF_KEY
empty_consts.description: Zero-initialized PBKDF key buffer. Provided as a convenience allocation target; must never be used as actual key material.

byte_aliases.name: PbkdfKey
byte_aliases.description: A 32-byte symmetric key derived from a password via PBKDF. Use as key material for AEAD ciphers or other symmetric operations.

functions.name: pbkdf
functions.description: Derives a 32-byte symmetric key from a password and salt using a password-based key derivation function with an explicit iteration count. Higher iteration counts increase resistance to brute-force attacks at the cost of derivation time.

params.name: password
params.description: The user-supplied password bytes to derive from.

params.name: salt
params.description: A per-credential random salt, typically 16+ bytes, stored alongside the derived key material. Prevents precomputation attacks across credentials.

params.name: iters
params.description: Number of PBKDF iterations. Higher values increase security against offline attacks; choose based on acceptable latency for the deployment context.

functions.name: salted_pbkdf
functions.description: Derives a 32-byte key from a password and salt using a library-defined default iteration count. Use when you do not need to control iteration count explicitly.

params.name: password
params.description: The user-supplied password bytes.

params.name: salt
params.description: A per-credential random salt.

functions.name: pbkdf_with_iters
functions.description: Derives a 32-byte key from a password using an explicit iteration count but no external salt. Suitable for contexts where a salt is embedded in or derived from the password itself.

params.name: password
params.description: The user-supplied password bytes.

params.name: iters
params.description: Number of PBKDF iterations.

functions.name: simple_pbkdf
functions.description: Derives a 32-byte key from a password using library defaults for both salt and iteration count. Provided for convenience in low-security or testing contexts; prefer salted_pbkdf or pbkdf in production.

params.name: password
params.description: The user-supplied password bytes.

sz_consts.name: RNG_SEED_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the seed used to initialize an AloeRng instance.

empty_consts.name: EMPTY_RNG_SEED
empty_consts.description: Zero-initialized RNG seed buffer. Provided as a convenience allocation target to fill before constructing an AloeRng; a zero seed must never be used directly.

byte_aliases.name: RngSeed
byte_aliases.description: A 32-byte seed value used to initialize an AloeRng. Must be sourced from a cryptographically secure entropy source (e.g. getrandom).

structs.name: AloeRng
structs.description: A deterministic, seedable pseudorandom number generator based on ChaCha20. Supports independent parallel streams via the stream parameter and arbitrary seek via word_pos, enabling reproducible and partitioned randomness across subsystems.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: The 32-byte seed that initializes the ChaCha20 key schedule. Determines the entire output sequence for a given stream and position.

fields.name: stream
fields.description: Stream identifier (ChaCha20 nonce component). Different stream values produce independent, non-overlapping output sequences from the same seed. Use distinct stream values to partition randomness between subsystems.

fields.name: word_pos
fields.description: Current 128-bit word position within the ChaCha20 output stream. Allows seeking to an arbitrary position for parallel or resumed generation.

structs.name: AloeRngU8Result
structs.description: Result of generating a single random u8 value. Returns the updated RNG state alongside the generated byte, supporting a functional update pattern.

fields.name: _self
fields.description: The updated AloeRng state after generating the value.

fields.name: next
fields.description: The generated random u8 value.

structs.name: AloeRngU32Result
structs.description: Result of generating a single random u32 value.

fields.name: _self
fields.description: The updated AloeRng state after generating the value.

fields.name: next
fields.description: The generated random u32 value.

structs.name: AloeRngU64Result
structs.description: Result of generating a single random u64 value.

fields.name: _self
fields.description: The updated AloeRng state after generating the value.

fields.name: next
fields.description: The generated random u64 value.

structs.name: AloeRngU128Result
structs.description: Result of generating a single random u128 value.

fields.name: _self
fields.description: The updated AloeRng state after generating the value.

fields.name: next
fields.description: The generated random u128 value.

traits.name: IAloeRng
traits.description: Builder and constructor interface for AloeRng. Allows configuration of seed, stream, and word position before use, supporting both fresh construction and fine-grained stream partitioning.

functions.name: with_seed
functions.description: Replaces the current seed and resets the RNG state accordingly. Returns a reference to self to support method chaining.

params.name: seed
params.description: A new 32-byte seed value from a CSPRNG.

functions.name: with_stream
functions.description: Sets the stream identifier, producing a logically independent output sequence from the same seed. Returns a reference to self for chaining. Use distinct stream values to partition randomness between concurrent subsystems without seed duplication.

params.name: stream
params.description: A u64 stream identifier. Each unique value yields a non-overlapping output sequence.

functions.name: with_word_pos
functions.description: Seeks the RNG to an arbitrary word position in the output stream. Useful for parallel generation or resuming from a known offset. Returns a reference to self for chaining.

params.name: word_pos
params.description: The 128-bit word offset to seek to within the ChaCha20 output stream.

functions.name: new
functions.description: Constructs a new AloeRng from the given seed, using default stream (0) and word position (0).

params.name: seed
params.description: A 32-byte seed from a cryptographically secure entropy source.

sz_consts.name: RECOVERY_PRIVKEY_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the recoverer's private key component. Fixed at 32 bytes.

sz_consts.name: RECOVERABLE_CIPHER_SZ
sz_consts.description: Size in bytes of the KEM ciphertext stored in a RecoveryKey. Aliased from MLKEM_512_CIPHER_SZ.

empty_consts.name: EMPTY_RECOVERY_PRIVKEY
empty_consts.description: Zero-initialized recovery private key buffer. Provided as a convenience allocation target to fill before constructing a RecoverableSecret; a zero key must never be used directly.

byte_aliases.name: RecoverySecret
byte_aliases.description: A 32-byte value representing either the recoverer's private key component or the derived recoverable secret. All instances must be sourced from a cryptographically secure entropy source or derived via the recovery hash construction.

byte_aliases.name: RecoveryCipher
byte_aliases.description: A KEM ciphertext produced during secret creation. Held by the recoverer and presented to the authorizer to obtain the shared secret needed for recovery. Treat as non-secret but integrity-sensitive.

structs.name: RecoveryKey
structs.description: The recoverer's credential bundle, produced during secret creation and held exclusively by the recoverer. Contains both the KEM ciphertext to present to the authorizer and the private key component to complete recovery. Neither field alone is sufficient to recover the secret.

fields.name: cipher
fields.description: The ML-KEM-512 ciphertext encapsulating the authorizer's shared secret contribution. Presented to the authorizer during recovery to obtain the decapsulated secret. Non-secret but must be stored faithfully — loss is unrecoverable.

fields.name: mac
fields.description: HMAC presented to authorizer to authenticate request

fields.name: secret
fields.description: The recoverer's 32-byte private key component. Combined with the authorizer's decapsulated secret to derive the final recovered secret. Must be kept confidential by the recoverer.

structs.name: RecoverableSecret
structs.description: The output of secret creation. Contains the derived secret and the recovery key needed to reconstruct it later. The secret holder retains the secret field for use; the recovery_key is handed to the recoverer for custody.

fields.name: secret
fields.description: The 32-byte derived secret, produced by hashing the KEM shared secret together with the recoverer's private key component. This is the value the secret holder uses.

fields.name: recovery_key
fields.description: The RecoveryKey bundle to be handed to the recoverer. Contains everything the recoverer needs to initiate and complete recovery given authorizer cooperation.

traits.name: IRecoverableSecret
traits.description: Construction and recovery interface for RecoverableSecret. Separates the creation path (requires authorizer public key) from the recovery path (requires authorizer cooperation and recoverer private key), ensuring neither party can recover the secret unilaterally.

functions.name: create
functions.description: Generates a new recoverable secret bound to the given authorizer. Internally generates a fresh KEM PRK seed and recoverer private key, encapsulates against the authorizer's public key, and derives the secret via H(kem_secret || recovery_private_key). The authorizer learns nothing at creation time.

params.name: authorizer
params.description: The authorizer's ML-KEM-512 encapsulator (public key wrapper). Only the public key is required; the authorizer's private key is not involved in secret creation.

params.name: recovery_secret
params.description: A pre generated 32-byte secret to use for the recovery key secret

params.name: prk_seed
params.description: A 32-byte random key to seed kyber encapsulation

params.name: ikm
params.description: Input Key Material for cipher mac

params.name: domain_info
params.description: Domain info for cipher mac

functions.name: recover
functions.description: Reconstructs the secret from an authorizer-supplied decapsulated shared secret and the recoverer's private key component. Mirrors the derivation in create: H(authorization || recovery_secret). Both inputs are required; neither alone is sufficient.

params.name: authorization
params.description: The MlKemSecret obtained by the authorizer decapsulating the cipher from the RecoveryKey. Represents the authorizer's explicit cooperation in the recovery event.

params.name: recovery_secret
params.description: The recoverer's private key component from RecoveryKey.secret. Must match the value generated at creation time.

params.name: authorizer
params.description: Authorizer KEM decapsulator

params.name: cipher
params.description: KEM ciphertext presented to authorizer for decapsulation

params.name: mac
params.description: HMAC presented to authorizer to authenticate request

params.name: ikm
params.description: Input Key Material for cipher_mac

params.name: domain_info
params.description: Domain info for cipher mac
